name: Jeri Southern
abstract: Jeri Southern (born Genevieve Lillian Hering; August 5, 1926 – August 4, 1991) was an American jazz singer and pianist.

works_created.t: I Don't Know Where to Turn

about_quotes.speaker: Mandel Johnny
about_quotes.evidence: I always like the Billie Holiday, Peggy Lee, Carmen McRae, good straight-forward singers that are very musical, Jeri Southern, I mean these are the kinds of singers that personally I like the best.
about_quotes.doc_title: Mandel Johnny — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Terry Gibbs
about_quotes.evidence: That was one show. ... Chet Baker’s group and Jeri Southern. ... I was the only white person on the whole show.
about_quotes.doc_title: Terry Gibbs interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2001 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Lorraine Feather
about_quotes.evidence: my friend was going there, Jeri Southern’s daughter Kathryn King.
about_quotes.doc_title: Lorraine Feather interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, March 22, 2023
